hooks.name: Post-Task Diagnostics
hooks.description: After a spec task completes, runs getDiagnostics on the .rs files the task changed. Terminal-free (no cargo), so it is safe under the shared bash session and cannot collide with a running build. Catches compile/type errors per task instead of waiting for the end-of-session agentStop check. Does NOT run fmt/clippy/tests — those stay gated to rust-quality-check.

action.prompt:
A spec task just completed. Verify it left no compile errors, WITHOUT touching the terminal beyond a single git diff.

1. Run `git diff --name-only HEAD -- '*.rs'` to list changed Rust files (ignore the spurious shared-terminal `Exit Code -1` artifact — read the listed paths).

2. If no `.rs` files changed → say nothing, stop.

3. Run `getDiagnostics` on the changed `.rs` files (max 10). Do NOT run `cargo build`, `cargo clippy`, or `cargo test` — getDiagnostics is sufficient and terminal-free.

4. Report ONLY if errors are found (file + message). If all clean → say nothing.

Rationale: cargo runs are centralized through the rust-quality-check sub-agent to avoid shared-terminal collisions; this hook is the cheap per-task safety net.
